mechanism_narrative: Positive retail-sales momentum reflects rising household demand and near-term earnings support; higher 3-month growth in RSAFS should raise S&P 500 forward 20-day returns relative to periods of flat or falling sales momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth has a nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in unemployment signals improving income security and lower recession risk; when UNRATE falls decisively below its recent trend, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns should be positive.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below its 6-month moving average by at least 0.3 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate, or if mean triggered returns are not higher at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates remain pinned near zero while CPI accelerates, equity investors price a future tightening shock and higher discount rates; the joint condition should lower S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 65% of cases or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production indicates normalization of output and operating leverage for cyclical firms; when INDPRO growth turns strongly positive, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns should improve.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO rebounds are followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least half of rolling 5-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in housing starts while unemployment remains elevated signals weakening household formation and credit-sensitive demand; this joint stress condition should reduce S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month percent change crosses below -10% while UNRATE > 5.0
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger is not associated with lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than either HOUST weakness or high UNRATE alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate signals tighter financial conditions and a higher discount rate for equities; once FEDFUNDS begins a clear upward crossing, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns should be negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses above +0.25 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if tightening triggers have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases and the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Large payroll gains with unemployment still above normal identify a recovery phase where earnings expectations improve without immediate wage-overheating pressure; the joint signal should raise 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-and-UNRATE interaction has nonpositive predictive contribution for 20-day returns after controlling for PAYEMS growth alone in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly CPI acceleration creates near-term rate-volatility and margin-pressure fears; when CPIAUCSL monthly growth crosses a high threshold, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns at or above the unconditional positive-return frequency across rolling 3-year windows.
